description:
You are working as a junior log-analyst.  
  A small Apache access log has been copied to  
  
    /home/user/logs/local_access.log  
  
  The file already exists before you start.  
  It contains **only IPv4 addresses** (no hostnames) and looks exactly like this:  
  ```
  127.0.0.1 - - [10/Sep/2023:12:00:00 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1024
  127.0.0.1 - - [10/Sep/2023:12:00:05 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 302 512
  127.0.0.1 - - [10/Sep/2023:12:00:10 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 200 2048
  ```
  (All three lines are already present in the file; do **not** change or rename it.)

  Your manager wants a quick reverse-DNS overview of every **unique** IP address that appears in the log.  
  Produce that overview as a CSV file named  
  
    /home/user/logs/local_ip_hostname_mapping.csv  

  Requirements for the CSV:

  1. The very first line is a header that reads exactly:  
     ```
     ip,hostname
     ```
  2. Every following line is a mapping in the form  
     ```
     <ip>,<hostname>
     ```
     • Use one comma `,` as the only delimiter.  
     • Do **not** add any spaces before or after the comma.  
  3. Sort the data lines lexicographically by the IP address (ascending).  
  4. Use Unix LF (`\n`) line endings and make sure the file ends with a trailing newline.  
  5. Rely on standard Linux tools (`host`, `dig -x`, `getent hosts`, etc.) for the reverse lookup; no hard-coding.  
  6. If a lookup fails, write `<unresolved>` (lower-case, no quotes) in the hostname column.  
     (Not needed for the given sample, but keep the rule in mind for robustness.)

  After you finish, the file **/home/user/logs/local_ip_hostname_mapping.csv** must exist and conform exactly to the format above so that an automated test can parse it.

  Hints (not commands):
  • Use whatever one-liners you prefer; no more than a couple of commands are really necessary.  
  • Remember to extract only *unique* IP addresses from the log before performing the reverse lookup.  
  • `getent hosts <ip>` is usually the simplest tool because it consults the local resolver setup (including /etc/hosts).
truth:
The file **/home/user/logs/local_access.log** is pre-created with the three lines shown in the public task description.

  Standard Debian/Ubuntu `/etc/hosts` in our container already contains the line  
  ```
  127.0.0.1   localhost
  ```
  so a reverse lookup on 127.0.0.1 will always return “localhost”.

  The grading script will therefore expect **/home/user/logs/local_ip_hostname_mapping.csv** to contain exactly two lines (including the header) with this content and a trailing newline:

  ```
  ip,hostname
  127.0.0.1,localhost
  ```

  (i.e. 32 bytes total:  
  `69 70 2c 68 6f 73 74 6e 61 6d 65 0a 31 32 37 2e 30 2e 30 2e 31 2c 6c 6f 63 61 6c 68 6f 73 74 0a`)
name: task_000000_a4e2e318
